name: Béla Bartók
abstract: Béla Viktor János Bartók (; Hungarian: [ˈbɒrtoːk ˈbeːlɒ]; 25 March 1881 – 26 September 1945) was a Hungarian composer, pianist and ethnomusicologist. He is considered one of the most important composers of the 20th century; he and Franz Liszt are regarded as Hungary's greatest composers.

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Spottswood
about_quotes.evidence: I liked classical music. I loved Claudio Monteverdi’s operas and masses and I liked Bela Bartok string quartets.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Spottswood interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 13, 2023

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: I missed a chance to meet him... So I missed the opportunity to meet Bela Bartok.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: James Chirillo
about_quotes.evidence: he turned me on to Béla Bartok, he was learning some “Mikrokosmos” at the time.
about_quotes.doc_title: James Chirillo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Jim Hall
about_quotes.evidence: I discovered Béla Bartók, and I did a lot of writing and composing music... I’m sure that it seeped in there just like my discovering Béla Bartók in music school (laughs) did
about_quotes.doc_title: Jim Hall — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: I didn't get too involved in the concepts of some of the composers... I didn't want my music to stretch in that direction.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: George Handy
about_quotes.evidence: Then Ravel, Bartok. The moderns, at the beginning, were the people that really stirred me up, that really got me thinking.
